steps.title: Find the scale factor between the two radii
steps.content:
The radius of circle A is $2x$ and the radius of circle B is $94x$. To compare the areas, start with the ratio of the radii:
$$k = \frac{\text{radius B}}{\text{radius A}} = \frac{94x}{2x} = 47$$

steps.title: Apply the area scaling rule
steps.content:
When a figure is scaled by a linear factor of $k$, its area is scaled by $k^2$. Here, the radius of circle B is 47 times the radius of circle A, so its area is:
$$47^2 = 2209$$
times the area of circle A.
